note: Group: Preposition group (after preposition other than 'de'). Gender/Number: Feminine plural. Used for feminine plural nouns after a preposition other than 'de'. Here, 'raisons' is feminine plural, and the preposition is 'pour'.
exampleFrench: Les raisons pour lesquelles nous sommes parties sont confidentielles.
exampleEnglish: The reasons for which we left are confidential.
imagePrompt: A woman whispering secrets to another, with confidential documents in the background.

note: Group: Preposition group (after preposition other than 'de'). Gender/Number: Masculine singular. Used for masculine singular nouns after a preposition other than 'de'. Here, 'sac' is masculine singular, preposition 'dans'.
exampleFrench: Elle a un sac à main dans lequel elle garde son argent.
exampleEnglish: She has a handbag in which she keeps her money.
imagePrompt: A woman holding a handbag with money inside, close-up on the bag.

note: Group: Preposition group (after preposition other than 'de'). Gender/Number: Masculine plural. Used for masculine plural nouns after a preposition other than 'de'. Here, 'hommes' is masculine plural, preposition 'entre'.
exampleFrench: Les deux hommes entre lesquels je me trouvais ne me parlaient pas.
exampleEnglish: The two men between whom I was standing were not talking to me.
imagePrompt: Two men standing apart in a crowd, not interacting.

note: Group: Preposition group (after preposition other than 'de'). Gender/Number: Feminine singular. Used for feminine singular nouns after a preposition other than 'de'. Here, 'chaise' is feminine singular, preposition 'sur'.
exampleFrench: La chaise sur laquelle je suis assise est inconfortable.
exampleEnglish: The chair on which I am sitting is uncomfortable.
imagePrompt: A person sitting on an uncomfortable chair, looking annoyed.

note: Group: Locution prépositive group (with prepositional phrases starting with 'de'). This is 'de' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Masculine singular. Used with prepositional phrases starting with 'de' (e.g., 'à bord de'). Here, 'bateau' is masculine singular.
exampleFrench: Voici le bateau à bord duquel j’ai traversé le fleuve.
exampleEnglish: Here is the boat on board which I crossed the river.
imagePrompt: A boat on a river, with someone on board looking across.

note: Group: Locution prépositive group (with prepositional phrases starting with 'de'). This is 'de' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Masculine plural. Used with prepositional phrases starting with 'de' for masculine plural nouns. Here, 'chemins' is masculine plural, 'le long de'.
exampleFrench: Les chemins le long desquels nous avons marché étaient en terre.
exampleEnglish: The paths along which we walked were dirt roads.
imagePrompt: People walking along dirt paths in a rural area.

note: Group: Proposition relative group (when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à'). This is 'à' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Masculine singular. Used when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à'. Here, 'pense' (penser à), 'garçon' is masculine singular.
exampleFrench: Le garçon auquel pense tout le temps est déjà fiancé à une autre.
exampleEnglish: The boy about whom she thinks all the time is already engaged to another.
imagePrompt: A girl thinking about a boy who is with another girl.

note: Group: Proposition relative group (when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à'). This is 'à' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Feminine plural. Used when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à' for feminine plural nouns. Here, 'choses' is feminine plural, 'tenir à'.
exampleFrench: Dans la vie, il y a peu de choses auxquelles nous devons tenir.
exampleEnglish: In life, there are few things to which we must hold.
imagePrompt: A person holding onto precious items in life.

note: Group: Proposition relative group (when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à'). This is 'à' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Masculine plural. Used when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à' for masculine plural nouns. Here, 'événements' is masculine plural, 'faire référence à'.
exampleFrench: Les événements auxquels vous faites référence sont regrettables.
exampleEnglish: The events to which you are referring are regrettable.
imagePrompt: People discussing past events with regret.

note: Group: Proposition relative group (when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à'). This is 'à' plus the first group form. Gender/Number: Feminine singular. Used when the relative clause verb is constructed with 'à' for feminine singular nouns. Here, 'entreprise' is feminine singular, 'consacrer à'.
exampleFrench: L’entreprise à laquelle nous avons consacré tant d’efforts est menacée.
exampleEnglish: The company to which we have devoted so much effort is threatened.
imagePrompt: Workers looking worried about their company's future.
